proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of the local average treatment effect at a regression-discontinuity cutoff when the running variable is subject to bounded manipulation, with the manipulation magnitude bounded by a McCrary-style density-discontinuity envelope and a covariate-conditional manipulation-share bound, recovering Lee 2008 sharp RD point identification at the trivial bound and characterizing the tightest sharp interval as a function of the discontinuity-magnitude and covariate-share parameters
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue axis remains doc/API.md §3, where the ThmSmith PartialID catalogue was empty at seed time and had no RD manipulation-envelope, covariate-cap, or equality-budget trimming proposal. The revised kernel is not a relabelling of the banked dynamic-IV partial-identification proposals because it concerns cutoff-side contaminated distributions and RD trimming geometry, not finite compliance histories.","published_axis":"The closest published result is GerardRokkanenRothe2020, which gives sharp RD manipulation bounds under exact density-ratio share logic, covariate conditioning, and fuzzy-RD share optimization. The revised gap is narrower and cleaner: the proposal studies a sensitivity envelope formed by a McCrary-style global upper bound plus external covariate-cell caps, and Conjecture 3 is now a positive open-set separation from the cell-cap-free GRR fixed-share envelope rather than a refutation of an unnamed shortcut. Theorem 2 has been restricted to the equality-budget face matching Lee2009 and the sharp-design GRR exact-share corner."}
seed_list: Prior work GerardRokkanenRothe2020 would imply that a one-sided density jump can be converted into an exact manipulator share and then into Lee-type RD bounds. Our seed conjectures that when the jump is only a McCrary envelope and covariates impose cellwise share caps, the sharp interval is a coupled budgeted trimming program whose endpoint can bind on a covariate cell rather than the global density jump.

Prior work ChoiLee2023 would predict that density continuity alone is too weak to validate RD. Our seed conjectures that density and covariate discontinuities jointly define a sharp feasibility frontier: some observed laws are impossible under any retained nonmanipulator law satisfying the share caps, so diagnostics become quantitative falsification inequalities.

Prior work CanayKamat2018 establishes that Lee-style local randomization implies continuity of predetermined covariate distributions. Our seed conjectures that the minimal amount of right-side trimming needed to restore covariate continuity is itself a sharp lower bound on manipulation, independent of outcomes.

Prior work Semenova2025 establishes covariate-conditioned Lee bounds under conditional monotonicity. Our seed conjectures that in RD manipulation, conditioning can widen or empty the identified set because the retained right-side covariate distribution must match the left-side cutoff law, a constraint absent from sample-selection Lee bounds.

Prior work McCrary2008 would imply that the density jump is the primary manipulation diagnostic. Our seed conjectures that a zero or statistically small density jump can still yield nontrivial partial-ID bounds once covariate-share caps force trimming within imbalanced cells.

Prior work HsuShiuWan2024 tests fuzzy RD LATE identifying assumptions. Our seed conjectures that bounded manipulation creates a first-stage positivity phase transition: the LATE interval becomes unbounded or sign-indeterminate before the density envelope is exhausted.

Prior work Lee2009 establishes that monotone sample selection sharp bounds are obtained by trimming outcome tails. Our seed conjectures that fuzzy RD manipulation trims an affine outcome-minus-lambda-treatment score, not the outcome alone, because the Wald denominator is endogenous to the removed mass.

Prior work HorowitzManski1995 would frame manipulated RD as contaminated data with a global contamination fraction. Our seed conjectures that the RD cutoff and covariate-continuity restrictions make the sharp set strictly smaller than the generic contamination interval through retained-covariate-law matching.

Prior work IshiharaSawada2024 would predict point identification under low-level harmless/detectable manipulation restrictions. Our seed conjectures that when those restrictions fail by a bounded amount, the sharp partial-ID set has a Lee face at zero violation and a Manski face at large violation.

Prior work BugniCanay2021 provides density-continuity testing via order statistics. Our seed conjectures that finite-sample density confidence bands can be mapped monotonically into a manipulation-envelope curve whose population limit is the sharp interval proposed here.

Prior work CattaneoTitiunik2022 positions validation and falsification as central RD practice. Our seed conjectures that the separate density and covariate diagnostics admit a single sharp trimming functional, making the diagnostic output directly causal rather than merely advisory.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GerardRokkanenRothe2020 would imply that a one-sided density jump can be converted into an exact manipulator share and then into Lee-type RD bounds. Our seed conjectures that when the jump is only a McCrary envelope and covariates impose cellwise share caps, the sharp interval is a coupled budgeted trimming program whose endpoint can bind on a covariate cell rather than the global density jump.

anchor_paper.author: Gerard, Rokkanen, and Rothe
anchor_paper.claim: Derives sharp treatment-effect bounds in RD designs with one-sided manipulation, using the density jump to infer the extent of manipulation.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift from exact share to envelope; not blocked because the mechanism is a new active-constraint phase transition, not a mere assumption weakening.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from GerardRokkanenRothe2020 because their sharp Lee trimming can be run at every candidate share in the envelope.
seed_details.rebuttal: False gap: covariate-share caps and retained-covariate-law matching couple candidate shares across cells, so the envelope endpoint is not a scalar union of exact-share trims.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChoiLee2023 would predict that density continuity alone is too weak to validate RD. Our seed conjectures that density and covariate discontinuities jointly define a sharp feasibility frontier: some observed laws are impossible under any retained nonmanipulator law satisfying the share caps, so diagnostics become quantitative falsification inequalities.

anchor_paper.author: Choi and Lee
anchor_paper.claim: Argues density continuity is neither necessary nor sufficient for RD validity and calls for attention to behavioral reactions around thresholds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist; this turns diagnostics into a sharp feasibility theorem.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from CanayKamat2018 because covariate jumps already falsify local randomization.
seed_details.rebuttal: CanayKamat2018 gives a testable implication, not the minimum trimming/share frontier restoring compatibility under bounded manipulation.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CanayKamat2018 establishes that Lee-style local randomization implies continuity of predetermined covariate distributions. Our seed conjectures that the minimal amount of right-side trimming needed to restore covariate continuity is itself a sharp lower bound on manipulation, independent of outcomes.

anchor_paper.author: Canay and Kamat
anchor_paper.claim: Develops approximate permutation tests for RD using implications of local randomization, including covariate-distribution continuity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation from test to sharp manipulation lower bound; not blocked because the new object is an identified manipulation-share frontier hidden in the test formulation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from total variation distance between left and right covariate laws.
seed_details.rebuttal: The total-variation lower bound is a candidate formula, but sharpness with right-side-only trimming and density-envelope compatibility still requires a formal coupling theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Semenova2025 establishes covariate-conditioned Lee bounds under conditional monotonicity. Our seed conjectures that in RD manipulation, conditioning can widen or empty the identified set because the retained right-side covariate distribution must match the left-side cutoff law, a constraint absent from sample-selection Lee bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Semenova
anchor_paper.claim: Generalizes Lee bounds by allowing the selection-response sign to be identified by pretreatment covariates.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Heterogeneity/covariate variation; not blocked because the surprising mechanism is that conditioning can harm feasibility via retained-law matching, not merely sharpen bounds.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from generalized Lee bounds because covariate cells are handled separately there.
seed_details.rebuttal: False gap: RD manipulation adds a cross-cell retained-covariate-law and global density budget, so cells cannot be solved independently.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work McCrary2008 would imply that the density jump is the primary manipulation diagnostic. Our seed conjectures that a zero or statistically small density jump can still yield nontrivial partial-ID bounds once covariate-share caps force trimming within imbalanced cells.

anchor_paper.author: McCrary
anchor_paper.claim: Proposes a density-discontinuity test as evidence of manipulation of the running variable.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens density-test pass/fail interpretation; not blocked because the phenomenon is a zero-density-jump but covariate-binding interval, a sign/regime change in diagnostic content.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from covariate balance tests in Lee2008 and CanayKamat2018.
seed_details.rebuttal: Those tests reject or fail to reject; they do not characterize a sharp causal interval under a zero global density envelope plus positive cell-specific caps.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HsuShiuWan2024 tests fuzzy RD LATE identifying assumptions. Our seed conjectures that bounded manipulation creates a first-stage positivity phase transition: the LATE interval becomes unbounded or sign-indeterminate before the density envelope is exhausted.

anchor_paper.author: Hsu, Shiu, and Wan
anchor_paper.claim: Derives testable implications of LATE identification assumptions in fuzzy RD designs.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift; not blocked because the phase transition concerns denominator feasibility under bounded manipulation, not a routine fuzzy-RD extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from weak-instrument logic because the Wald denominator can approach zero.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap is the sharp manipulation-envelope threshold at which feasible trimming can drive the nonmanipulator first stage to zero.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Lee2009 establishes that monotone sample selection sharp bounds are obtained by trimming outcome tails. Our seed conjectures that fuzzy RD manipulation trims an affine outcome-minus-lambda-treatment score, not the outcome alone, because the Wald denominator is endogenous to the removed mass.

anchor_paper.author: Lee
anchor_paper.claim: Gives sharp treatment-effect bounds under monotone sample selection by trimming outcome tails.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation to fuzzy Wald ratio; not blocked because it reveals an endpoint-score change hidden by mean-effect Lee bounds.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from fractional linear programming for ratios.
seed_details.rebuttal: The mathematical tool is standard, but the RD manipulation object and exact reduction from Lee trimming to affine-score trimming is not stated in the RD manipulation literature.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HorowitzManski1995 would frame manipulated RD as contaminated data with a global contamination fraction. Our seed conjectures that the RD cutoff and covariate-continuity restrictions make the sharp set strictly smaller than the generic contamination interval through retained-covariate-law matching.

anchor_paper.author: Horowitz and Manski
anchor_paper.claim: Develops identification and robustness bounds for contaminated and corrupted data.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition; not blocked because the claim isolates the retained-covariate-law equation as the source of strict tightening beyond generic contamination.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from adding linear restrictions to a contaminated-data LP.
seed_details.rebuttal: The general LP fact is known, but the RD-specific covariate-law equation and its strictness relative to Horowitz-Manski is the theorem-level object.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work IshiharaSawada2024 would predict point identification under low-level harmless/detectable manipulation restrictions. Our seed conjectures that when those restrictions fail by a bounded amount, the sharp partial-ID set has a Lee face at zero violation and a Manski face at large violation.

anchor_paper.author: Ishihara and Sawada
anchor_paper.claim: Provides low-level conditions for manipulation-robust RD identification and diagnostic density tests.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift; not blocked because the seed claims an interpolating sharp-set geometry between point-ID and no-information faces.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a generic sensitivity-analysis interpolation and not a new RD theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed faces are anchored to RD density and covariate continuity, but this seed is weaker because the anchor is not yet a published journal article.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BugniCanay2021 provides density-continuity testing via order statistics. Our seed conjectures that finite-sample density confidence bands can be mapped monotonically into a manipulation-envelope curve whose population limit is the sharp interval proposed here.

anchor_paper.author: Bugni and Canay
anchor_paper.claim: Develops a density-continuity test for RD using g-order statistics.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends test output to causal sensitivity curve; blocked as a standalone seed unless paired with a new population sharpness theorem, so retained only as a lower-ranked implementation-facing angle.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is inference plumbing after the population identified set is known.
seed_details.rebuttal: True for novelty; the seed is not chosen as the kernel and only supports downstream implementation.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CattaneoTitiunik2022 positions validation and falsification as central RD practice. Our seed conjectures that the separate density and covariate diagnostics admit a single sharp trimming functional, making the diagnostic output directly causal rather than merely advisory.

anchor_paper.author: Cattaneo and Titiunik
anchor_paper.claim: Reviews RD designs and emphasizes validation/falsification alongside identification and inference.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - synthesis seed; not chosen because it is less theorem-specific than the GerardRokkanenRothe2020 anchored kernel.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a reframing of existing diagnostics, not a publishable theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: As stated it is too broad; the chosen seed sharpens it into a concrete budgeted trimming theorem.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Lee2008 (Journal of Econometrics, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2007.05.004) establishes local randomization under imprecise control and predicts covariate continuity at the cutoff; tension: small failures are treated as diagnostics, not quantitative sensitivity inputs.
HahnToddVanDerKlaauw2001 (Econometrica) gives the classical RD and fuzzy RD identification benchmark; open gap: no bounded-manipulation identified set.
McCrary2008 (Journal of Econometrics, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2007.05.005) develops the density-discontinuity manipulation test; tension: the density jump is used as pass/fail evidence rather than an envelope for causal bounds.
CattaneoJanssonMa2020 (JASA) modernizes density-discontinuity estimation; phenomenon: practical rddensity output could feed a manipulation envelope but existing identification theory rarely does so.
CanayKamat2018 (Review of Economic Studies) formalizes covariate-distribution continuity implications of Lee-style local randomization; open gap: translating covariate imbalance into a sharp manipulation-share restriction.
GerardRokkanenRothe2020 (Quantitative Economics, https://doi.org/10.3982/QE1079) gives sharp RD manipulation bounds when one-sided manipulation identifies the share from the density ratio; tension: empirical density evidence is often an interval/envelope, not an exact share.
Lee2009 (Review of Economic Studies) gives sharp trimming bounds under monotone sample selection; mechanism: endpoint trimming is the natural geometry for manipulated-right-side RD laws.
HorowitzManski1995 (Econometrica) gives contaminated/corrupted-data robustness bounds; closest generic partial-ID ancestor for unknown removed mass.
Semenova2025 (Journal of Econometrics) generalizes Lee bounds with covariate-conditional monotonicity; open gap: no RD density-envelope and retained-covariate-law constraint.
ChoiLee2023 (Pacific Economic Review, https://doi.org/10.1111/1468-0106.12380) argues density continuity is neither necessary nor sufficient for RD validity; phenomenon: need to model behavior around the cutoff instead of relying on density tests alone.
IshiharaSawada2024 (arXiv:2009.07551) gives manipulation-robust RD point-identification conditions; gap: bounded violations and sharp partial-ID intervals.
AraiHsuKitagawaMourifieWan2022 (Quantitative Economics) and HsuShiuWan2024 (Journal of Econometrics) test fuzzy RD identifying assumptions; tension: LATE denominators can become weak under bounded manipulation.
